title: Use race-archetype metadata for strategy priors
summary: Undercut and overcut strength depends on degradation, warm-up difficulty, overtaking difficulty, and track position.
rationale: Undercut and overcut strength depends on degradation, warm-up difficulty, overtaking difficulty, and track position. Race name is currently mostly treated as a category; explicit circuit archetype features can help models generalize across years and rare drivers.
implementation_hint: Create a small manual Race metadata table: street/temporary circuit, high degradation, low warm-up/overcut-friendly, overtaking difficulty, typical pit-loss band, and wet-prone flag. Interact these with TyreLife, Compound, Position, LapsRemaining_Est, and field-density features.
expected_effect: May improve transfer to sparse race-year combinations and sharpen pit-window timing where categorical Race_Year frequency is too blunt.
risk: Manual metadata can inject noise if the synthetic data does not follow real F1 behavior; keep it low-dimensional and ablate as a feature block.
